title: E7-MT-041-EditorActionsAccessKit

scope.summary:
GOAL: Expose every interactive editor action in both the native code editor (E1 cluster) and the native rich-text editor (E2 cluster) through the WP-011 AccessKit model-accessibility surface, each with a stable author_id, correct AccessKit role, and declared actions. An out-of-process swarm agent must be able to discover and invoke any editor action (format text, insert snippet/block, open find/replace, toggle find options, navigate find matches, save document, toggle multi-cursor, jump to line, open command palette, open language picker) purely through the AccessKit action channel — no screen-scraping, no keyboard simulation.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE:
  CREATE  src/frontend/native_editors/accessibility/editor_action_registry.rs
    — defines EditorActionId enum (one variant per action, kebab-case string repr) and EditorActionNode struct { author_id: String, role: AccessKitRole, label: String, actions: Vec<AccessKitAction>, state: EditorActionState }
    — defines EditorActionRegistry: maintains a stable BTreeMap<EditorActionId, EditorActionNode> per open editor pane; emits diff notifications to the WP-011 AccessKit adapter on change
  EDIT    src/frontend/native_editors/code_editor/pane.rs
    — on pane init and on state change: register/update each code-editor action node in the EditorActionRegistry
    — map: save=CustomAction{"save"}, find_open=CustomAction{"find-open"}, find_next=CustomAction{"find-next"}, find_prev=CustomAction{"find-prev"}, find_toggle_case=ToggleButton, find_toggle_word=ToggleButton, find_toggle_regex=ToggleButton, replace_open=CustomAction{"replace-open"}, replace_one=CustomAction{"replace-one"}, replace_all=CustomAction{"replace-all"}, format=CustomAction{"format"}, go_to_line=CustomAction{"go-to-line"}, multi_cursor_add=CustomAction{"multi-cursor-add"}, multi_cursor_clear=CustomAction{"multi-cursor-clear"}, command_palette_open=CustomAction{"command-palette-open"}, language_picker_open=CustomAction{"language-picker-open"}
  EDIT    src/frontend/native_editors/rich_editor/pane.rs
    — same registry call for rich-text editor actions: save, find_open, find_next, find_prev, find_toggle_case, find_toggle_word, find_toggle_regex, replace_one, replace_all, format_bold, format_italic, format_code, format_heading_1..6, insert_block_slash_command, command_palette_open
  CREATE  tests/native_editors/e7_editor_action_accesskit.rs
    — kittest harness that opens both editor panes, queries the AccessKit tree, asserts every EditorActionId appears with the correct author_id prefix, role, and at least one declared action; simulates action dispatch and asserts state updates

FILES TO CREATE/EDIT (all under allowed paths):
  src/frontend/native_editors/accessibility/editor_action_registry.rs  (NEW)
  src/frontend/native_editors/code_editor/pane.rs                      (EDIT)
  src/frontend/native_editors/rich_editor/pane.rs                      (EDIT)
  src/frontend/native_editors/accessibility/mod.rs                      (EDIT — add pub mod editor_action_registry)
  tests/native_editors/e7_editor_action_accesskit.rs                   (NEW)

DEPENDENCIES / UNBLOCK CONDITIONS:
  - WP-KERNEL-011 MT-001 spike delivered: egui + AccessKit adapter exists in src/frontend/shell/accessibility.rs (or equivalent); the adapter exposes `register_node(author_id, role, actions)` and `dispatch_action(author_id, action_id)` on a stable public API.
  - WP-KERNEL-012 MT-001 (code editor pane skeleton) and MT-011 (rich-text editor pane skeleton) are COMPLETE so pane.rs exists with an init/update lifecycle hook to attach to.
  - WP-KERNEL-012 MT-031 (shared command bus) is COMPLETE so the code-editor and rich-editor actions already fire through the bus; this MT adds AccessKit registration on top of the bus dispatch.

RISK IF MISSED: MT-043 (SwarmEditProof) cannot execute: the out-of-process agent test has no AccessKit node to target for either editor, making the E7 cluster proof impossible. MT-044 parity proof cannot include model-accessibility acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: AC-041-01: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend --test e7_editor_action_accesskit` passes with zero failures. The test must open both panes in-process, query the AccessKit tree, and assert that every EditorActionId variant maps to an AccessKit node with the correct author_id (format: 'editor.<pane_type>.<action_id>'), correct role, and at least one declared action.

AC-041-02: Every code-editor action listed in implementation note IN-041-03 is present in the AccessKit tree with a stable author_id that does NOT include runtime-generated random segments; the author_id must be deterministic given (pane_type, action_id, pane_instance_index).
AC-041-03: Every rich-text editor action listed in implementation note IN-041-04 is present in the AccessKit tree with the same deterministic author_id convention.

AC-041-04: Simulating `dispatch_action('editor.code.find-open', 'activate')` via the AccessKit adapter in the kittest harness causes the find panel to open (observable as a new AccessKit node 'editor.code.find-panel' appearing in the tree within 100 ms).

AC-041-05: Simulating `dispatch_action('editor.rich.format-bold', 'activate')` causes the bold format to apply to the current selection (observable via the AccessKit node state 'editor.rich.format-bold' having `checked: true` when the cursor is inside bold text).

AC-041-06: Simulating `dispatch_action('editor.code.save', 'activate')` triggers the backend save API call (observable via a test spy on the knowledge document update endpoint, or an EventLedger event if spy is unavailable).
AC-041-07: `cargo build -p handshake_frontend` succeeds with zero new warnings introduced by this MT.
AC-041-08: No mock or placeholder substitutes any AccessKit node that a real editor widget would supply; every node in the kittest corresponds to a real interactive widget that is rendered.
proof_targets: PROOF-041-A: Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend --test e7_editor_action_accesskit 2>&1`; paste full stdout. Every line must show 'ok'. Zero 'FAILED' lines.

PROOF-041-B: In the kittest, print the full AccessKit tree to stdout and paste it. Reviewer must be able to locate 'editor.code.find-open', 'editor.code.save', 'editor.rich.format-bold', 'editor.rich.command-palette-open' as named nodes.
PROOF-041-C: Run `cargo build -p handshake_frontend 2>&1 | grep -E 'warning:|error:'`; paste output. Zero new warnings attributable to files changed in this MT.
PROOF-041-D: Paste the AccessKit node dump for at least 5 find-panel actions showing correct role (Button or ToggleButton as appropriate) and at least one declared action string.
implementation_notes: IN-041-01: AccessKit integration point. The WP-KERNEL-011 spike chose egui + AccessKit. egui's AccessKit adapter is in `egui_accesskit::AccessKitPlugin` (or the equivalent shell adapter). Each egui widget that calls `ui.add(widget)` can be given a stable `Id` via `egui::Id::new('editor.code.find-open')` and then the AccessKit node is derived from that Id. For custom action dispatch (actions that are not native egui interactions), use the `egui_accesskit` custom-action API: call `response.ctx.set_accesskit_action_handler(id, action_id, handler)`. If the WP-011 adapter wraps this in a higher-level `register_node` API, use that API; do NOT duplicate the egui Id/AccessKit glue.

IN-041-02: author_id convention. Format: `editor.<pane_type>.<action_id>` where pane_type is `code` or `rich`, and action_id is the kebab-case action name. When multiple editor panes of the same type are open simultaneously, suffix with `.<instance_index>` (0-based). The instance_index is stable for the lifetime of the pane and is assigned by the EditorActionRegistry at pane registration time. Example: `editor.code.find-open`, `editor.code.find-open.1` for a second code pane.

IN-041-03: Code editor action set (all must appear in AccessKit tree). Map each to the egui widget or custom-action node it wraps:
  editor.code.save                — Button (Ctrl+S handler)
  editor.code.find-open           — Button (opens FindReplacePanel; see React ref FindReplacePanel.tsx)
  editor.code.find-next           — Button (visible only when find panel open; same React ref)
  editor.code.find-prev           — Button
  editor.code.find-toggle-case    — ToggleButton (aria-pressed = case_sensitive state)
  editor.code.find-toggle-word    — ToggleButton
  editor.code.find-toggle-regex   — ToggleButton
  editor.code.replace-open        — Button (shows replace row)
  editor.code.replace-one         — Button
  editor.code.replace-all         — Button
  editor.code.format              — Button (format document via LSP or built-in formatter)
  editor.code.go-to-line          — Button (opens go-to-line input)
  editor.code.multi-cursor-add    — Button (add cursor at AccessKit-specified position)
  editor.code.multi-cursor-clear  — Button
  editor.code.command-palette-open — Button (opens CommandPalette; see React ref CommandPalette.tsx)
  editor.code.language-picker-open — Button (opens language selector; see React ref MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx data-testid=monaco-code-block-language)

IN-041-04: Rich-text editor action set (all must appear in AccessKit tree):
  editor.rich.save                — Button
  editor.rich.find-open           — Button
  editor.rich.find-next           — Button
  editor.rich.find-prev           — Button
  editor.rich.find-toggle-case    — ToggleButton
  editor.rich.find-toggle-word    — ToggleButton
  editor.rich.find-toggle-regex   — ToggleButton
  editor.rich.replace-one         — Button
  editor.rich.replace-all         — Button
  editor.rich.format-bold         — ToggleButton (checked = cursor in bold)
  editor.rich.format-italic       — ToggleButton
  editor.rich.format-code         — ToggleButton (inline code)
  editor.rich.format-heading-1    — Button
  editor.rich.format-heading-2    — Button
  editor.rich.format-heading-3    — Button
  editor.rich.format-heading-4    — Button
  editor.rich.format-heading-5    — Button
  editor.rich.format-heading-6    — Button
  editor.rich.insert-slash-command — Button (opens slash-command picker; inserts block)
  editor.rich.command-palette-open — Button

IN-041-06: kittest harness pattern. Use egui_kittest (the in-process UI test harness from the WP-011 toolkit). Instantiate a test app that includes both a code editor pane and a rich-text editor pane with synthetic content. After one frame, query ctx.accesskit_node_ids() or the equivalent AccessKit tree dump. Assert node presence by author_id string. For action dispatch, call ctx.perform_accesskit_action(node_id, action_id). Observe post-dispatch state by querying the AccessKit tree again after one frame.

IN-041-07: React parity references for this MT (READ ONLY — do not edit):
  app/src/components/FindReplacePanel.tsx — stable test IDs: find-panel, find-input, find-count, find-next, find-prev, find-toggle-case, find-toggle-word, find-toggle-regex, find-error, replace-input, replace-one, replace-all, find-close
  app/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx — CommandPaletteAction shape: { id, label, description, keywords, disabled, stableId, testId }
  app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx — data-testid=monaco-code-block-language for the language picker
  app/src/components/DocumentView.tsx — shows save/command-palette/find integration in the legacy doc view
The native implementation must expose the same logical actions; the author_id naming replaces the React testId/stableId convention.

IN-041-08: HBR-SWARM compliance. Every AccessKit node registered by this MT must be stable across frames (same author_id, same role) so a swarm agent's stored node reference remains valid between frames. Do not regenerate author_ids on re-render. The EditorActionRegistry is the single source of truth for node identity.

IN-041-09: Do not emit AccessKit node updates on every frame if the state has not changed. Compute a hash of EditorActionState before calling notify_accesskit_adapter; skip the push if the hash is unchanged. This keeps the AccessKit diff stream quiet during steady-state rendering (HBR-QUIET).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/FindReplacePanel.tsx — find/replace action surface, option toggles, match count; port the logical action set and stable selector naming convention to AccessKit author_ids
app/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx — command palette open/close/search/run action; port CommandPaletteAction.stableId convention to AccessKit author_id
app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx — language picker action (data-testid=monaco-code-block-language), format action, multi-cursor; port to code editor AccessKit nodes
app/src/components/DocumentView.tsx — save action, command palette trigger (Ctrl+K / Ctrl+Shift+P), find request surface
binds_backend_api: handshake_core: POST /knowledge/documents/{id}/save (or equivalent Tauri IPC command `knowledge_document_save`) — triggered by editor.code.save and editor.rich.save actions
No new backend APIs introduced by this MT; AccessKit surface is purely frontend

hbr_obligations: HBR-INT: kittest in tests/native_editors/e7_editor_action_accesskit.rs must pass in CI; all AC must be green before MT is DONE
HBR-SWARM: every AccessKit node must have a stable author_id (deterministic, no random segments) so swarm agents can store and reuse node references across frames
HBR-VIS: AccessKit tree dump printed in kittest is the visual proof surface; the test must print the tree to stdout so a reviewer can read it without running the test themselves
HBR-QUIET: do not push AccessKit diff updates on every frame when state is unchanged; hash-check before push

risks: RISK-041-01: author_id instability — egui generates internal Ids based on widget insertion order; if pane layout changes (e.g. a panel added above the editor), all downstream widget Ids shift and stored swarm agent references become stale.
RISK-041-02: AccessKit not available in headless test — if kittest does not initialize the AccessKit adapter, the entire test class silently passes with an empty tree, producing a false green.
RISK-041-03: ToggleButton state not updated — format-bold and find-toggle-case nodes may report stale 'checked' state if the registry update call is missing from the cursor-move handler.

RISK-041-04: Action dispatch does not reach the editor — if the AccessKit adapter dispatches to the egui action queue but the editor pane does not consume the queue before the next frame, the action is silently dropped.

RISK-041-05: Multiple pane instance collision — if two code panes open simultaneously and both use author_id 'editor.code.find-open' (without instance suffix), the AccessKit tree has duplicate nodes and agents cannot distinguish them.

RISK-041-06: Save action calls wrong backend endpoint — the code editor and rich editor use different backend API surfaces (knowledge_documents vs loom_blocks); wiring both to the same Tauri IPC handler will silently corrupt rich-text saves.
minimum_controls: CTRL-041-01: Use `egui::Id::new(author_id_string)` (stable string-based Id) for every AccessKit widget, never `ui.id().with(index)` which shifts on layout change. Assert in the kittest that all expected author_ids are present after a layout change (add a dummy panel, re-render, re-query).

CTRL-041-02: Assert in the kittest that the AccessKit tree is NON-EMPTY after init; fail the test if the tree root has zero children. Add a canary node 'editor.accesskit.health' that is always present.
CTRL-041-03: In the kittest, move the cursor into and out of bold text and assert that 'editor.rich.format-bold' node has checked=true/false respectively.
CTRL-041-04: After dispatching an action in the kittest, advance one frame and assert the state change; if the state has not changed, fail the test with a descriptive message, not a timeout.

CTRL-041-05: The EditorActionRegistry appends the instance_index suffix when registering a pane and asserts uniqueness; if a duplicate author_id is detected, panic in debug builds and log an error in release builds.

CTRL-041-06: Save action dispatch must be routed through the existing MT-037/MT-038 backend wiring layer (E6 cluster), not via a new direct call. Assert in the kittest that the correct backend command (knowledge_document_save vs knowledge_rich_document_save) is invoked based on pane type.
